title: Admin login grants access to the dashboard
description: This test verifies that a valid admin login lets the user access the protected admin dashboard.

steps.description: Navigate to /admin
Fill in the username field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Verify the authenticated admin dashboard is displayed

title: Admin first login requires a password change
description: This test verifies that a first-time admin login with a forced password change routes the user into the password-change flow before dashboard access.

steps.description: Navigate to /admin
Fill in the username field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Verify the password-change flow is displayed

title: ML training page shows status and starts a training run
description: This test verifies that an authenticated admin can review the current training status and trigger a training run from the training page.

steps.description: Navigate to /admin
Fill in the username field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item for the ML training page
Wait for the training status to load
Click the control to trigger a training run
Verify the training status is displayed
Verify a training submission confirmation is visible

title: ML training page reflects an updated training state after submission
description: This test verifies that triggering training from the ML training page updates the visible training state after the request is submitted.

steps.description: Navigate to /admin
Fill in the username field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item for the ML training page
Wait for the training status to load
Click the control to trigger a training run
Wait for the page to refresh the training state
Verify the updated training status is displayed

title: Admin dashboard navigation opens the signals backtest page
description: This test verifies that an authenticated user can move from the dashboard to the signals backtest page through the internal navigation.

steps.description: Navigate to /admin
Fill in the username field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item for the signals backtest page
Verify the signals backtest page is displayed

title: View the default signals backtest results
description: An authenticated admin can open the signals backtest page and see historical performance results by default.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in {{LOGIN_USER}} into the username field
Fill in {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the form
Click the link to open the signals backtest page
Verify historical backtest results are displayed
Verify the backtest table is visible

title: Admin dashboard navigation opens the ML overview page
description: This test verifies that an authenticated user can move from the dashboard to the ML overview page through the internal navigation.

steps.description: Navigate to /admin
Fill in the username field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item for the ML overview page
Verify the ML overview page is displayed

title: Filter signals backtest results by date
description: An authenticated admin can narrow signals backtest results by choosing a different date range and see the table update.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in {{LOGIN_USER}} into the username field
Fill in {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the form
Click the link to open the signals backtest page
Select a different date range in the backtest filters
Verify refreshed backtest results are displayed
Verify the displayed results reflect the selected date range

title: Filter signals backtest results by horizon
description: An authenticated admin can narrow signals backtest results by selecting a different horizon and see the table update.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in {{LOGIN_USER}} into the username field
Fill in {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the form
Click the link to open the signals backtest page
Select a different horizon in the backtest filters
Verify refreshed backtest results are displayed
Verify the displayed results reflect the selected horizon

title: Update both signals backtest filters in one session
description: An authenticated admin can change both date and horizon filters on the signals backtest page and see the results refresh to match the combined criteria.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in {{LOGIN_USER}} into the username field
Fill in {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the form
Click the link to open the signals backtest page
Select a different date range in the backtest filters
Select a different horizon in the backtest filters
Verify refreshed backtest results are displayed
Verify the displayed results reflect the selected filters

title: Admin dashboard navigation opens the algorithm page
description: This test verifies that an authenticated user can move from the dashboard to the algorithm page through the internal navigation.

steps.description: Navigate to /admin
Fill in the username field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item for the algorithm page
Verify the algorithm page is displayed

title: Admin dashboard navigation opens the calibration page
description: This test verifies that an authenticated user can move from the dashboard to the calibration page through the internal navigation.

steps.description: Navigate to /admin
Fill in the username field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item for the calibration page
Verify the calibration page is displayed

title: Admin dashboard navigation opens the Elo page
description: This test verifies that an authenticated user can move from the dashboard to the Elo page through the internal navigation.

steps.description: Navigate to /admin
Fill in the username field with {{LOGIN_USER}}
Fill in the password field with {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}
Submit the login form
Click the navigation item for the Elo page
Verify the Elo page is displayed

title: Keep signals backtest results usable after changing filters repeatedly
description: An authenticated admin can change backtest filters more than once and still see a usable results table after the final selection.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in {{LOGIN_USER}} into the username field
Fill in {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the form
Click the link to open the signals backtest page
Select a different date range in the backtest filters
Select another valid date range in the backtest filters
Select a different horizon in the backtest filters
Verify backtest results are still displayed
Verify the table remains populated after filter changes

title: Stay on the signals backtest page while switching filters
description: An authenticated admin can switch between filter options on the signals backtest page without losing access to the results view.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in {{LOGIN_USER}} into the username field
Fill in {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the form
Click the link to open the signals backtest page
Select a different horizon in the backtest filters
Select a different date range in the backtest filters
Verify updated backtest results are displayed
Verify the results table remains visible

title: Invalid admin login shows a failure message
description: This test verifies that incorrect credentials are rejected and the user sees a login failure state.

steps.description: Navigate to /admin
Fill in the username field with fake-admin
Fill in the password field with fake-password
Submit the login form
Verify a login failure message is visible

title: Unauthenticated access to the admin area is blocked
description: This test verifies that opening a protected admin route without a session does not expose admin content and sends the user to sign in.

steps.description: Navigate to /admin/ml
Verify the login screen is displayed
Verify admin content is not visible

title: See an empty state when no signals backtest data matches the filters
description: An authenticated admin who applies a restrictive backtest filter sees an empty state instead of broken results when no rows match.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Fill in {{LOGIN_USER}} into the username field
Fill in {{LOGIN_PASSWORD}} into the password field
Submit the form
Click the link to open the signals backtest page
Select a date range with no matching historical results
Verify an empty state message is visible
Verify no backtest rows are displayed
